lesson.title: State Space Models
lesson.body: A state space model has two equations: the state transition x_t = Φx_{t-1} + w_t (w_t ~ N(0, Q)) and the observation equation y_t = A_tx_t + v_t (v_t ~ N(0, R)). The Kalman filter recursively estimates the hidden state x_t given observations y_1:t, minimizing mean squared error. The filter alternates between prediction and update steps.

rooms.question: In a state space model, the observation equation y_t = Ax_t + v_t represents:
answers: A noisy measurement of the hidden state
rooms.explanation: The observation equation relates observable y_t to unobservable state x_t with measurement noise v_t.

rooms.question: The Kalman filter prediction step gives:
answers: State estimate and covariance before seeing y_t
rooms.explanation: Prediction step: propagate the state mean and covariance forward in time before observing y_t.

rooms.question: The Kalman gain K_t determines:
answers: How much to trust the new observation vs the prediction
rooms.explanation: K_t balances prior prediction uncertainty P vs measurement noise R. Large K → trust observation; small K → trust prediction.

rooms.question: Order the Kalman filter recursion steps at each time t:
items: Update state: x_{t|t} = x_{t|t-1} + K_t(y_t − Ax_{t|t-1})
rooms.explanation: Kalman filter: predict → compute gain → update state → update covariance. Then move to t+1.

rooms.question: The Kalman smoother (RTS smoother) differs from the Kalman filter in that it:
answers: Uses all T observations to estimate each x_t (uses future data)
rooms.explanation: The filter gives x_{t|t} (using y_1,...,y_t). The smoother gives x_{t|T} (using all y_1,...,y_T), which is more accurate but only for offline processing.

boss.name: The Kalman Daemon
boss.taunt: Your true state is hidden. But I can infer it from your noisy signals.

questions.question: The local level model (random walk + noise) as a state space model is:
questions.latex: x_t = x_{t-1} + w_t, \quad y_t = x_t + v_t
answers: x_t = x_{t-1} + w_t; y_t = x_t + v_t
questions.explanation: The local level model: the 'true level' x_t follows a random walk, and we observe it with noise v_t. Kalman filter estimates x_t = exponential smoothing.

questions.question: If the measurement noise variance R is very large relative to Q, the Kalman gain K_t approaches:
questions.explanation: Large R means measurements are noisy → K_t → 0 → heavily discount observations. The filter sticks with its predictions.

questions.question: ARIMA models can be written in state space form. This is useful because:
answers: It allows handling missing data and time-varying parameters via the Kalman filter
It reduces the number of parameters
It converts ARIMA to a neural network
questions.explanation: State space representation of ARIMA enables: Kalman filter for missing values, online estimation, and a unified framework for all linear Gaussian models.
